title: potentilla inclinata var. incisoserrata
intro: Potentilla inclinata var. incisoserrata, also known as the alpine cinquefoil, is a plant species belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is commonly found in high-altitude mountainous regions. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. Alpine cinquefoil has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, offering benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
